frameworks.name: efficient coding / neural coding precision
frameworks.one_line: Surround context dynamically reallocates sensory encoding precision around the inducer orientation.

frameworks.name: Bayesian observer model
frameworks.one_line: Local encoding change is fed into a Bayesian decoder to predict the perceptual bias.

frameworks.name: natural scene statistics of orientation
frameworks.one_line: Baseline (no-surround) encoding mirrors the natural prior over orientation.

core_question: What neural mechanism produces the orientation tilt illusion induced by an oriented surround?
key_assumptions: Sensory encoding precision can be read out from combined psychophysical and fMRI data
A Bayesian observer linking encoding to behavior is appropriate
Cortical receptive-field geometry constrains where surround modulation acts
manipulations: Presence vs absence of an oriented surround around a center grating
Orientation of the center stimulus relative to the surround
Visual area along the ventral stream (early vs higher)
key_findings: Without surround, encoding precision tracks natural orientation statistics
With surround, precision is selectively boosted for orientations near the surround
Local encoding change predicts the tilt illusion via a Bayesian observer model
Surround modulation grows along the ventral stream and localizes to center-surround boundary RFs
interpretations: The tilt illusion arises from context-driven reallocation of coding resources at the contextual boundary
Behavioral and neural signatures of the illusion share a common encoding-precision origin
